bridge.description: Two parallel tracks — professional and academic — running simultaneously.

fasterSolutions.description: The gap between knowing how to code and writing code that ships is wider than any classroom suggests. This summer closed it.

magneto.description: Nearly two years on Colombia's leading HR platform — real traffic, real teams, real tradeoffs. Where best practices stopped being theory and started being survival.

cafeto.description: Joined a mature UI library powering airline SaaS products — the kind of codebase that teaches you more than a greenfield ever could. Refactoring, migrating, and learning how good teams handle technical debt.

arroyo.description: First real team, first real deadline pressure. Worked on TimeTracker — an internal hour-logging platform — and discovered that clean requirements are a luxury, not a given.

platzi.description: Nine months going deep — React, Redux, GraphQL, and enough architecture thinking to know why the choices matter. The year that reframed how I approach a problem.

teinco.description: Formal grounding in systems and software development — the academic layer that gives the hands-on experience somewhere to anchor.

uninpahu.description: 6th semester — the part of engineering school where the theory starts connecting to everything you've already shipped. AgroTech, an IoT agriculture system, got university recognition in the first semester.

lakeSuperior.description: Two years in Minnesota — studying in English, thinking in systems, learning to work in rooms where nobody looked like me. The foundation everything else is built on.

smartAgriculture.title: Smart Agriculture System
smartAgriculture.description: Arduino-based automated irrigation prototype using soil moisture sensors. Designed scalable architecture for crop data collection and water optimization. Received university recognition, first semester 2024.

gcpCert.title: Google Cloud Associate Cloud Engineer
gcpCert.description: Certified in cloud infrastructure, compute, storage, and IAM on Google Cloud Platform. Practical experience with Compute Engine, Cloud Storage, and identity management.

terminal.approach1: → Requirements first. Functional and non-functional — both matter.
terminal.approach2: Understanding the business domain before the tech stack.
terminal.methodology1: → Scrum by default. Sprint planning, daily, review, retro.
terminal.methodology2: Kanban when the work doesn't fit in boxes.
terminal.tooling1: → AI-augmented, not AI-dependent. Copilot and LLMs accelerate —
terminal.tooling2: but the architectural decisions stay human.
terminal.collab1: → Backlog is a conversation. Close to product, close to the team.
terminal.collab2: Ambiguity is a problem to solve, not to ship.

codeBlock.webVitals: "small PRs, fast feedback, no big bangs"

understand.label: Understand
understand.description: Map functional and non-functional requirements. Ask what the business actually needs — not just what was written in the ticket.

architect.label: Design
architect.description: Define the contract before the code. Data flow, component boundaries, API shape — on paper first.

build.label: Build
build.description: Iterative and incremental. Small PRs, atomic commits, continuous feedback from the team and the backlog.

ship.label: Deliver
ship.description: Code review, QA handoff, sprint demo. Delivery is a team event — not a solo push to main.

reflect.label: Improve
reflect.description: Retrospective thinking applied beyond the ceremony. What slowed us down, what can be automated, what should be documented.

cta.label: What's next
cta.heading: Now see the full technical picture.

metadata.title: About
metadata.description: The story behind the developer — Sebastian Ballen's professional journey, technical approach, and what drives his work as a Full-Stack Developer and Google Cloud Engineer.
